'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references />== See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also! == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}.
