'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year }}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year }}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year }} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year }}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク}}

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}}.
== Overview ==

The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year }}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=draft}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|12=draft}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=draft}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|12=draft}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=draft}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=draft}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=draft}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|12=draft}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|12=draft}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|12=draft}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|12=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|12=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|12=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|12=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|12=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|12=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|12=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|12=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|12=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|12=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|12=draft}}
{{Aviation-stub}}

Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|12=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|12=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|12=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|12=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|12=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|12=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|12=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|12=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|12=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=draft}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|12=draft}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=draft}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|12=draft}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=draft}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=draft}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|12=draft}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=draft}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|12=draft}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|12=draft}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|12=draft}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|12=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|12=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|12=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|12=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|12=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|12=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|12=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|12=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|12=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|12=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|12=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|12=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|12=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|12=draft}} {{Aviation-stub}} <!--Sky Day-->

Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Aviation-stub}}

[[sn:Sky Day]] [[ja:空の日]] [[en:Sky Day]]
<!--Sky Day-->

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Aviation-stub}}

revision.comment: 44 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:空の日 up to rev 102398789
revision.text:
'''空の日'''（そらのひ）は日本で制定された記念日で、[[9月20日]]である。
== 概要 == [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に[[徳川好敏]]、[[日野熊蔵]]両[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[大尉]]が[[代々木公園|代々木練兵場]]において[[日本]]初の動力飛行に成功して30周年、ならびに[[紀元二千六百年記念行事|紀元2600年]]を記念して[[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年）[[9月28日]]に制定された「'''航空日'''」にその起源を持つ。
翌年「航空日」は9月20日と決定され、それが定着した。[[日本の降伏|終戦]]後一時廃止されたが[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に復活。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）には民間航空再開40周年を記念し「'''空の日'''」と改称され、また9月20日から[[9月30日|30日]]が「'''空の旬間'''」とされ今に至る。シンボルキャラクターの「くにまるくん」は「空の日」改称時に同時に設定され、「9（く）2（に）0（まる）」に因んで命名された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=www.mlit.go.jp|language=ja}}</ref>。
9月20日という日付に設定されたのには特別な意味はないが、[[3月10日]]の[[陸軍記念日]]や[[5月27日]]の[[海軍記念日]]が春に行われるために時期をずらして[[秋]]とし、その中から[[晴れ]]の[[特異日]]を選んだものである（なお徳川・日野の初飛行自体は[[12月19日]]であった）<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
毎年、「空の旬間」には全国の空港等のうち1か所がスカイフェスタ会場とされて大規模なイベントが行われる。この他にも[[航空局]]・[[航空会社]]・空港管理会社等が各地の空港・レーダー事務所等において、[[管制塔]]などの空港施設見学、航空教室等を開催する。[[成田山新勝寺]]（[[成田国際空港]]）や[[穴守稲荷神社]]（[[東京国際空港]]）、[[飛行神社]]などの航空産業と関わりの深い[[航空神社|社寺]]では、[[航空安全]]の[[祈祷]]式が執り行われる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺 |url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/ |website=www.naritasan.or.jp |access-date=2023-03-30}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列 |url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103 |website=Aviation Wire |access-date=2023-03-30 |language=ja-JP}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会 |url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D |website=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会 |access-date=2023-03-30 |language=ja |first=一般社団法人 |last=八幡市観光協会}}</ref>。
[[2003年]]から「[[敬老の日]]」が毎年[[9月15日]]から9月第3月曜日に変更されたことにより、[[2004年]]・[[2010年]]・[[2021年]]は「空の日」が祝日と被ることになる。
== 脚注 == <references />
==関連事項== *[[ミス日本]] - [[2011年]]まで、「ミス日本・空の日」という表彰項目があり、受賞者は[[国土交通省]]、[[防衛省]]主催・後援の航空の安全や保安を目的としたキャンペーン活動のイメージガールとしてアピールを行っていた。 *[[国際民間航空デー]] *[[飛行機の日]] - [[12月17日]]。ライト兄弟による世界初飛行を記念する日。 *[[日本人初飛行の日]] - [[12月19日]]。[[日野熊蔵]]・[[徳川好敏]]による公式な日本初飛行を記念する日。 *[[民間航空記念日]] - [[10月25日]]。[[日本航空]]による戦後の民間航空第一便の就航を記念する日。 *[[国際航空業務再開の日]] - [[2月2日]]。日本航空による国際線の再開を記念する日。 *[[世界一周記念日]] - [[3月6日]]。日本航空による世界一周西回り路線の就航を記念する日。 *[[スチュワーデスの日]] - [[3月5日]]。[[東京航空輸送社]]にて初のエアガールが採用された事を記念する日。 *[[リンドバーグ翼の日]] - [[5月21日]]。[[チャールズ・リンドバーグ]]による大西洋無着陸横断飛行を記念する日。 *[[バスの日]] - 運輸に関する同日の記念日。
==外部リンク== *[https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html 国土交通省航空局] *[https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 日本ニュース「航空日本三十年銀翼の祭典」]（1940年第1回「航空日」の記念イベントのニュース映像） *[http://www.soranohi.net/ 空の日ネット] *{{Kotobank}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:そらのひ}} [[Category:空]] [[Category:日本の航空]] [[Category:日本の記念日]] [[Category:9月の記念日]] [[Category:皇紀2600年]] {{Aviation-stub}}

== 概要 == [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に[[徳川好敏]]、[[日野熊蔵]]両[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[大尉]]が[[代々木公園|代々木練兵場]]において[[日本]]初の動力飛行に成功して30周年、ならびに[[紀元二千六百年記念行事|紀元2600年]]を記念して[[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年）[[9月28日]]に制定された「'''航空日'''」にその起源を持つ。
翌年「航空日」は9月20日と決定され、それが定着した。[[日本の降伏|終戦]]後一時廃止されたが[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に復活。[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）には民間航空再開40周年を記念し「'''空の日'''」と改称され、また9月20日から[[9月30日|30日]]が「'''空の旬間'''」とされ今に至る。シンボルキャラクターの「くにまるくん」は「空の日」改称時に同時に設定され、「9（く）2（に）0（まる）」に因んで命名された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=www.mlit.go.jp|language=ja}}</ref>。
9月20日という日付に設定されたのには特別な意味はないが、[[3月10日]]の[[陸軍記念日]]や[[5月27日]]の[[海軍記念日]]が春に行われるために時期をずらして[[秋]]とし、その中から[[晴れ]]の[[特異日]]を選んだものである（なお徳川・日野の初飛行自体は[[12月19日]]であった）<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958年（昭和33年）5月23日初版発行 [[西角井正慶]]編 東京堂出版</ref>。
毎年、「空の旬間」には全国の空港等のうち1か所がスカイフェスタ会場とされて大規模なイベントが行われる。この他にも[[航空局]]・[[航空会社]]・空港管理会社等が各地の空港・レーダー事務所等において、[[管制塔]]などの空港施設見学、航空教室等を開催する。[[成田山新勝寺]]（[[成田国際空港]]）や[[穴守稲荷神社]]（[[東京国際空港]]）、[[飛行神社]]などの航空産業と関わりの深い[[航空神社|社寺]]では、[[航空安全]]の[[祈祷]]式が執り行われる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺 |url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/ |website=www.naritasan.or.jp |access-date=2023-03-30}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列 |url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103 |website=Aviation Wire |access-date=2023-03-30 |language=ja-JP}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会 |url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D |website=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会 |access-date=2023-03-30 |language=ja |first=一般社団法人 |last=八幡市観光協会}}</ref>。
[[2003年]]から「[[敬老の日]]」が毎年[[9月15日]]から9月第3月曜日に変更されたことにより、[[2004年]]・[[2010年]]・[[2021年]]は「空の日」が祝日と被ることになる。
== 脚注 == <references />
==関連事項== *[[ミス日本]] - [[2011年]]まで、「ミス日本・空の日」という表彰項目があり、受賞者は[[国土交通省]]、[[防衛省]]主催・後援の航空の安全や保安を目的としたキャンペーン活動のイメージガールとしてアピールを行っていた。 *[[国際民間航空デー]] *[[飛行機の日]] - [[12月17日]]。ライト兄弟による世界初飛行を記念する日。 *[[日本人初飛行の日]] - [[12月19日]]。[[日野熊蔵]]・[[徳川好敏]]による公式な日本初飛行を記念する日。 *[[民間航空記念日]] - [[10月25日]]。[[日本航空]]による戦後の民間航空第一便の就航を記念する日。 *[[国際航空業務再開の日]] - [[2月2日]]。日本航空による国際線の再開を記念する日。 *[[世界一周記念日]] - [[3月6日]]。日本航空による世界一周西回り路線の就航を記念する日。 *[[スチュワーデスの日]] - [[3月5日]]。[[東京航空輸送社]]にて初のエアガールが採用された事を記念する日。 *[[リンドバーグ翼の日]] - [[5月21日]]。[[チャールズ・リンドバーグ]]による大西洋無着陸横断飛行を記念する日。 *[[バスの日]] - 運輸に関する同日の記念日。
==外部リンク== *[https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html 国土交通省航空局] *[https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 日本ニュース「航空日本三十年銀翼の祭典」]（1940年第1回「航空日」の記念イベントのニュース映像） *[http://www.soranohi.net/ 空の日ネット] *{{Kotobank}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:そらのひ}} [[Category:空]] [[Category:日本の航空]] [[Category:日本の記念日]] [[Category:9月の記念日]] [[Category:皇紀2600年]] {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Aviation-stub}}

In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|ja|コトバンク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 28|qq=}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|qq=}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kumazō|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's surrender in World War II|qq=}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 30|qq=}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 10|qq=}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 27|qq=}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Bureau|qq=}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miss Nippon|qq=}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2011|qq=}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Defense|qq=}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Civil Aviation Day|qq=}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 17|qq=}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 25|qq=}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 2|qq=}}. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.
