[[sn:Sky Day]] [[ja:空の日]] [[en:Sky Day]]
<!--Sky Day-->

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 28|qq=}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|qq=}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kumazō|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's surrender in World War II|qq=}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 30|qq=}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 10|qq=}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 27|qq=}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Bureau|qq=}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miss Nippon|qq=}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2011|qq=}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Defense|qq=}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Civil Aviation Day|qq=}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 17|qq=}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 25|qq=}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 2|qq=}}. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.

[[sn:Sky Day]] [[ja:空の日]] [[en:Sky Day]]
<!--Sky Day-->

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|ja|1940年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on {{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 28|qq=}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|ja|徳川好敏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|qq=}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|ja|日野熊蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kumazō|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Japan's surrender in World War II|ja|日本の降伏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's surrender in World War II|qq=}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|ja|1992年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} to {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 30|qq=}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|ja|9月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 20|qq=}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|March 10|ja|3月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 10|qq=}}) and {{ill|Navy Commemoration Day|ja|海軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Navy Commemoration Day|qq=}} ({{ill|May 27|ja|5月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 27|qq=}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Civil Aviation Bureau|ja|航空局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Bureau|qq=}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miss Nippon|qq=}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2011|qq=}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Defense|qq=}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Civil Aviation Day|qq=}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 17|qq=}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 25|qq=}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 2|qq=}}. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|qq=}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|ja|成田国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita International Airport|qq=}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|aviation safety|ja|航空安全|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/aviation safety|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|ja|2003年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|ja|敬老の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Respect for the Aged Day|qq=}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|ja|2004年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2004|qq=}}, {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}, and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|ja|ミス日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miss Nippon|qq=}} - Until {{ill|2011|ja|2011年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2011|qq=}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|ja|国土交通省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense|ja|防衛省|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Defense|qq=}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|ja|国際民間航空デー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Civil Aviation Day|qq=}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 17|ja|12月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 17|qq=}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - {{ill|December 19|ja|12月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 19|qq=}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - {{ill|October 25|ja|10月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 25|qq=}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - {{ill|February 2|ja|2月2日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 2|qq=}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=}} - {{ill|March 6|ja|3月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 6|qq=}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=}} - {{ill|March 5|ja|3月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 5|qq=}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=}} - {{ill|May 21|ja|5月21日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 21|qq=}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.

Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Commemorative days in Japan
revision.text:
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|空の日|version=102398789|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Sky]] [[Category:Aviation in Japan]]
[[Category:September observances]]
[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}

Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

<!--Sky Day--> [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. <!--Sky Day--> [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sky Day]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
