revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|qq=|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|空の日|version=102398789|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}

(The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.

Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].

Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|qq=|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|qq=|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|qq=|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].

<!--Sky Day--> [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11597337}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q11597337}} [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] [[Category:Aviation in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:September observances]] [[Category:Sky]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].

Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]].
