(The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}

<!--Sky Day--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11597337}} [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] [[Category:Aviation in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:September observances]] [[Category:Sky]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].

Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. [[ja:空の日]]
<!--Sky Day--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11597337}} [[Category:2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire]] [[Category:Aviation in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Observances in Japan]] [[Category:September observances]] [[Category:Sky]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on [[:en:September 20|September 20]].
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to [[:en:1940|1940]] (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on [[:en:September 28|September 28]] to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi]] and [[:en:Hino Kumazō|Hino Kumazō]], as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to [[:en:September 20|September 20]] and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following [[:en:Surrender of Japan|Japan's surrender in World War II]], it was reinstated in [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28). In [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from [[:en:September 20|September 20]] to [[:en:September 30|September 30]] was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date [[:en:September 20|September 20]] holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ([[:en:March 10|March 10]]) and [[:en:Navy Day|Navy Commemoration Day]] ([[:en:May 27|May 27]]), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on [[:en:December 19|December 19]])<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the [[:en:Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|Civil Aviation Bureau]], airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji Temple]] (near [[:en:Narita International Airport|Narita International Airport]]), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for [[:en:Aviation safety|aviation safety]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since [[:en:2003|2003]], when [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in [[:en:2004|2004]], [[:en:2010|2010]], and [[:en:2021|2021]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[:en:Miss Nippon|Miss Nippon]] - Until [[:en:2011|2011]], "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the [[:en:Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] and the [[:en:Ministry of Defense (Japan)|Ministry of Defense]]. * [[:en:International Civil Aviation Day|International Civil Aviation Day]] * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 17|December 17]]. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:December 19|December 19]]. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:October 25|October 25]]. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:February 2|February 2]]. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 6|March 6]]. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:March 5|March 5]]. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:May 21|May 21]]. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * [[:en:Kotobank|Kotobank]] {{Aviation-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|空の日|version=102398789|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597337]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}

Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]]
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}

'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Army Commemoration Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Masayoshi Nishitsunoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}}.

Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Bus Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]]
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28|1=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|1=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō|1=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II|1=Surrender of Japan}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30|1=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Q11658711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10|1=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day|1=Navy Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27|1=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19|1=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau|1=Japan Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|1=Narita-san}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport|1=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety|1=Aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon|1=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense|1=Ministry of Defense (Japan)}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day|1=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17|1=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19|1=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25|1=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2|1=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6|1=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5|1=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21|1=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Q11327637|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
== External Links == * [https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/index.html Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Civil Aviation Bureau] * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0001300402_00000&chapter=002 Japan News - Aviation Day (1940) Event Footage] * [http://www.soranohi.net/ Sky Day Net] * {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|WD=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}} {{Aviation-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|空の日|version=102398789|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:空の日]]
'''Sky Day''' (空の日, Sora no Hi) is a commemorative day established in Japan, observed on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}}.
== Overview ==
The origins of Sky Day date back to {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}} (Shōwa 15), when {{ill|Aviation Day|ja|航空日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}} was established on {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28|1=September 28}} to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Japan's first powered flight by {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|1=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}} and {{ill|Hino Kumazō|en|Hino Kumazō|WD=Q5767348|lt=Hino Kumazō|1=Hino Kumazō}}, as well as the {{ill|2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|ja|紀元二千六百年記念行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th Anniversary of the Japanese Imperial Era|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5366450}}.
The following year, Aviation Day was moved to {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} and became widely recognized. After being temporarily abolished following {{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|WD=Q6540361|lt=Japan's surrender in World War II|1=Surrender of Japan}}, it was reinstated in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28). In {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4), to commemorate the 40th anniversary of the resumption of civil aviation in Japan, it was renamed Sky Day. Additionally, the period from {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} to {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|WD=Q2881|lt=September 30|1=September 30}} was designated as {{ill|Sky Week|ja|空の旬間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Week|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, which continues to this day. The mascot {{ill|Kunimaru-kun|ja|くにまるくん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimaru-kun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空の日|1=Sky Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597337}}, introduced at the same time, was named after the phonetic reading of "9 (ku) 2 (ni) 0 (maru)"<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/03_information/02_sora/p03.html|title=シンボルキャラクター「くにまる」くんって？|accessdate=2018-09-24|website=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|language=ja}}</ref>.
The date {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} holds no specific significance but was chosen to complement {{ill|Army Commemoration Day|ja|陸軍記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Commemoration Day|1=Q11658711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658711}} ({{ill|March 10|en|March 10|WD=Q2397|lt=March 10|1=March 10}}) and {{ill|Navy Day|en|Navy Day|WD=Q3107490|lt=Navy Commemoration Day|1=Navy Day}} ({{ill|May 27|en|May 27|WD=Q2587|lt=May 27|1=May 27}}), which are held in spring. It was selected as an autumn observance and chosen from days known for clear weather. (The actual flight by Tokugawa and Hino occurred on {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19|1=December 19}})<ref>「年中行事事典」p295 1958th year （昭和33rd year ）5月23日初版発行 {{ill|Seikaku Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikaku Nishikado|1=Nishitsunoi Masayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629502}}編 東京堂出版</ref>.
Each year, during Sky Week, major events are held at airports nationwide, with one airport designated as the Sky Festival venue for large-scale celebrations. Additional activities organized by the {{ill|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|en|Japan Civil Aviation Bureau|WD=Q11613862|lt=Civil Aviation Bureau|1=Japan Civil Aviation Bureau}}, airlines, and airport management companies include facility tours (e.g., {{ill|control towers|ja|管制塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/control towers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Control tower|WD=Q918324}}) and aviation workshops. Temples and shrines related to the aviation industry, such as {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple|1=Narita-san}} (near {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|WD=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport|1=Narita International Airport}}), {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597221}} (near {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}}), and {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}}, hold special prayer ceremonies for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|WD=Q640853|lt=aviation safety|1=Aviation safety}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.naritasan.or.jp/2017/09/21/9555/|title=空の日記念／航空安全特別大祈祷会を厳修 – 大本山成田山新勝寺|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Naritasan Shinshoji Temple}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aviationwire.jp/archives/260103|title=穴守稲荷、「空の日」に航空安全祈願祭復興 航空会社も参列|accessdate=2023-03-30|website=Aviation Wire}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kankou-yawata.org/?event_news=%E9%A3%9B%E8%A1%8C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BB%8A%E9%80%B1%E3%81%AE%E8%8A%B1%E6%89%8B%E6%B0%B4%E3%81%A8%E3%80%8C%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%A5%E3%83%BB%E7%A9%BA%E3%81%AE%E6%97%AC%E9%96%93%E5%A5%89%E7%A5%9D|title=一般社団法人 八幡市観光協会|accessdate=2023-03-30}}</ref>.
Since {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}, when {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} was shifted to the third Monday of September, Sky Day occasionally coincides with the national holiday, as in {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}, {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}, and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|Miss Nippon|en|Miss Nippon|WD=Q2455781|lt=Miss Nippon|1=Miss Nippon}} - Until {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}, "Miss Nippon Sky Day" was an award category, and recipients served as goodwill ambassadors for aviation safety campaigns organized by the {{ill|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|WD=Q1376196|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} and the {{ill|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|en|Ministry of Defense (Japan)|WD=Q1062689|lt=Ministry of Defense|1=Ministry of Defense (Japan)}}. * {{ill|International Civil Aviation Day|en|International Civil Aviation Day|WD=Q633178|lt=International Civil Aviation Day|1=International Civil Aviation Day}} * {{ill|Airplane Day|ja|飛行機の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Airplane Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 17|en|December 17|WD=Q2413|lt=December 17|1=December 17}}. Commemorates the Wright brothers' first flight. * {{ill|Japan's First Flight Day|ja|日本人初飛行の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's First Flight Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|December 19|en|December 19|WD=Q2451|lt=December 19|1=December 19}}. Commemorates the first official flight in Japan by Hino Kumazō and Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. * {{ill|Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja|民間航空記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Civil Aviation Memorial Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25|1=October 25}}. Marks the resumption of civil aviation in Japan after World War II. * {{ill|International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja|国際航空業務再開の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International Aviation Operations Resumption Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|February 2|en|February 2|WD=Q2313|lt=February 2|1=February 2}}. Marks the resumption of Japan Airlines' international flights. * {{ill|Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja|世界一周記念日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Around-the-World Commemoration Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 6|en|March 6|WD=Q2399|lt=March 6|1=March 6}}. Celebrates the inauguration of Japan Airlines' westward around-the-world route. * {{ill|Stewardess Day|ja|スチュワーデスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stewardess Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|March 5|en|March 5|WD=Q2392|lt=March 5|1=March 5}}. Commemorates the hiring of Japan's first female flight attendants. * {{ill|Lindbergh Wing Day|ja|リンドバーグ翼の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lindbergh Wing Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|May 21|en|May 21|WD=Q2580|lt=May 21|1=May 21}}. Commemorates Charles Lindbergh's first solo transatlantic flight. * {{ill|Bus Day|ja|バスの日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus Day|1=Q11327637|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327637}} - Another commemorative day related to transportation.
